preamble: The shift from the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to the voiceless velar fricative [x] (as in 'loch') occurs in historical sound laws such as Grimm's Law and in specific dialectal clusters in Scots and Northern English.

examples.note: Historical development in Scots and Northern English where the dental fricative or its historical counterpart interacts with a following stop to produce a velar fricative.
Systemic parallels in the development of aspirated stops (Theta and Chi) into fricatives (θ and x).
